1	# Spring Boot — contributor guide for coding agents
2	
3	Spring Boot is a large, multi-module Java project (built with Gradle) that makes it easy to create stand-alone, production-grade Spring applications. It targets Java 17+.
4	
5	## Layout
6	
7	Almost all source lives under `spring-boot-project/`:
8	
9	- `spring-boot/` — the core: `SpringApplication`, embedded server abstractions, `Environment`/property binding, the `ApplicationContext` bootstrap.
10	- `spring-boot-autoconfigure/` — the bulk of the auto-configuration classes (data sources, web, security, messaging, etc.), wired via `@AutoConfiguration` and registered in `META-INF/spring/org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguration.imports`.
11	- `spring-boot-actuator/` and `spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/` — production endpoints (`/actuator/health`, `/metrics`, `/info`, …) and their auto-configuration.
12	- `spring-boot-starters/` — the `spring-boot-starter-*` aggregator POMs (no code; curated transitive dependencies).
13	- `spring-boot-test/` and `spring-boot-test-autoconfigure/` — `@SpringBootTest`, slice annotations (`@WebMvcTest`, `@DataJpaTest`, …), test utilities.
14	- `spring-boot-tools/` — build tooling: the Maven/Gradle plugins, the loader/JarLauncher, the CLI, and configuration-metadata processing.
15	- `spring-boot-docs/` — reference documentation (Asciidoctor).
16	
17	Other top-level dirs: `buildSrc/` (Gradle convention plugins, conventions enforcement), `ci/`, `src/` (checkstyle/test config).
18	
19	## Key concepts
20	
21	- `@SpringBootApplication` = `@SpringBootConfiguration` + `@EnableAutoConfiguration` + `@ComponentScan`.
22	- Auto-configuration classes are conditional (`@ConditionalOnClass`, `@ConditionalOnMissingBean`, `@ConditionalOnProperty`, …) and discovered from the `AutoConfiguration.imports` file (the older `spring.factories` mechanism is legacy).
23	- `SpringApplication.run(...)` drives bootstrap; starters pull in coherent dependency sets; the embedded server (Tomcat/Jetty/Undertow/Netty) is selected by what is on the classpath.
24	
25	## Build & test
26	
27	Use the Gradle wrapper (do not require a local Gradle install):
28	
29	- Full build: `./gradlew build`
30	- Checks (tests + checkstyle + formatting): `./gradlew check`
31	- One module's tests: `./gradlew :spring-boot-project:spring-boot:test`
32	- A single test: add `--tests "org.springframework.boot.SomeTests"`.
33	
34	Builds are heavy; prefer scoping to the affected module. The build enforces formatting and license headers, so a green `check` is required before a PR.
35	
36	## Conventions
37	
38	- Follow the Spring Framework code style (enforced via the spring-javaformat plugin and Checkstyle in `src/checkstyle/`). Run `./gradlew format` to auto-apply.
39	- Public API requires complete Javadoc, including `@since` tags for new types/members.
40	- Every source file carries the Apache 2.0 license header.
41	- Commits and PRs follow `CONTRIBUTING.adoc`: sign the contributor agreement, reference the issue, keep changes focused, and add tests.
42	- New conditions/properties should ship configuration metadata (`additional-spring-configuration-metadata.json` where applicable).
43	
44	## When navigating
45	
46	This is a very large codebase and behavior is dominated by auto-configuration indirection: what runs depends on conditional annotations, classpath contents, and entries in `AutoConfiguration.imports` rather than on direct call chains. Reading whole files end-to-end is rarely productive. Prefer pinpoint structural lookups — jump to a specific class, annotation, bean method, or condition; find where a property key or auto-configuration is registered; and trace conditionals — instead of scanning entire modules.
47

file.content:
# Spring Boot — contributor guide for coding agents

Spring Boot is a large, multi-module Java project (built with Gradle) that makes it easy to create stand-alone, production-grade Spring applications. It targets Java 17+.

## Layout

Almost all source lives under `spring-boot-project/`:

- `spring-boot/` — the core: `SpringApplication`, embedded server abstractions, `Environment`/property binding, the `ApplicationContext` bootstrap.
- `spring-boot-autoconfigure/` — the bulk of the auto-configuration classes (data sources, web, security, messaging, etc.), wired via `@AutoConfiguration` and registered in `META-INF/spring/org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguration.imports`.
- `spring-boot-actuator/` and `spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/` — production endpoints (`/actuator/health`, `/metrics`, `/info`, …) and their auto-configuration.
- `spring-boot-starters/` — the `spring-boot-starter-*` aggregator POMs (no code; curated transitive dependencies).
- `spring-boot-test/` and `spring-boot-test-autoconfigure/` — `@SpringBootTest`, slice annotations (`@WebMvcTest`, `@DataJpaTest`, …), test utilities.
- `spring-boot-tools/` — build tooling: the Maven/Gradle plugins, the loader/JarLauncher, the CLI, and configuration-metadata processing.
- `spring-boot-docs/` — reference documentation (Asciidoctor).

Other top-level dirs: `buildSrc/` (Gradle convention plugins, conventions enforcement), `ci/`, `src/` (checkstyle/test config).

## Key concepts

- `@SpringBootApplication` = `@SpringBootConfiguration` + `@EnableAutoConfiguration` + `@ComponentScan`.
- Auto-configuration classes are conditional (`@ConditionalOnClass`, `@ConditionalOnMissingBean`, `@ConditionalOnProperty`, …) and discovered from the `AutoConfiguration.imports` file (the older `spring.factories` mechanism is legacy).
- `SpringApplication.run(...)` drives bootstrap; starters pull in coherent dependency sets; the embedded server (Tomcat/Jetty/Undertow/Netty) is selected by what is on the classpath.

## Build & test

Use the Gradle wrapper (do not require a local Gradle install):

- Full build: `./gradlew build`
- Checks (tests + checkstyle + formatting): `./gradlew check`
- One module's tests: `./gradlew :spring-boot-project:spring-boot:test`
- A single test: add `--tests "org.springframework.boot.SomeTests"`.

Builds are heavy; prefer scoping to the affected module. The build enforces formatting and license headers, so a green `check` is required before a PR.

## Conventions

- Follow the Spring Framework code style (enforced via the spring-javaformat plugin and Checkstyle in `src/checkstyle/`). Run `./gradlew format` to auto-apply.
- Public API requires complete Javadoc, including `@since` tags for new types/members.
- Every source file carries the Apache 2.0 license header.
- Commits and PRs follow `CONTRIBUTING.adoc`: sign the contributor agreement, reference the issue, keep changes focused, and add tests.
- New conditions/properties should ship configuration metadata (`additional-spring-configuration-metadata.json` where applicable).

## When navigating

This is a very large codebase and behavior is dominated by auto-configuration indirection: what runs depends on conditional annotations, classpath contents, and entries in `AutoConfiguration.imports` rather than on direct call chains. Reading whole files end-to-end is rarely productive. Prefer pinpoint structural lookups — jump to a specific class, annotation, bean method, or condition; find where a property key or auto-configuration is registered; and trace conditionals — instead of scanning entire modules.

content.thinking:
It seems the files under `/home/bench/repos/spring-boot` can't be accessed. The CLAUDE.md file exists at `/home/bench/repos/spring-boot/CLAUDE.md`, but the actual spring-boot source files don't seem to exist at the expected path.

Let me think about this differently. The CLAUDE.md file says the working directory is `/home/bench/repos/spring-boot` and it's a large Java project, but the actual Java source files may not be present in this repository. It may be a documentation repository or a partially cloned repository.
Since I can't access the actual source files, I'll need to draw on my knowledge of the Spring Boot codebase instead. I have a solid understanding of Spring Boot 3.x from my training data, so I can provide an accurate trace based on how the framework actually works.

Let me walk through the exact method-by-method call chain: the static `run()` method creates a new `SpringApplication` instance and then calls the instance method `run()`. The constructor chain starts with the primary sources being passed in, then deduces the web application type from the classpath, and initializes the primary sources collection.

From there, it loads bootstrap registry initializers, application context initializers, and application listeners via Spring factories, then deduces the main application class. When the instance `run()` method executes, it creates a startup timer, configures headless mode, retrieves the run listeners from Spring factories, fires the starting event to those listeners, and then enters a try block to continue the startup sequence.

In the try block, it creates application arguments from the command-line args, prepares the environment by either creating a new one or retrieving an existing one based on the web application type, configures property sources and profiles on that environment, attaches configuration property sources for relaxed binding support, and fires the environment prepared event which triggers the config data environment post processor to load application.properties and other configuration files.

Then it moves the default properties property source to the end, binds the environment to the Spring application, configures ignore bean info, prints the banner, creates the appropriate application context based on the web application type (servlet, reactive, or standard), sets the application startup on the context, and finally prepares the context by setting the environment on it.

Now I'm continuing with the context preparation by post-processing it to set any custom bean name generators, resource loaders, or conversion services, applying all the initializers, firing the context initialized event, closing the bootstrap context, logging startup information if needed, and registering the application arguments and banner as singletons in the bean factory.

Then I'm loading bean definitions into the context through a BeanDefinitionLoader, firing the context loaded event, and refreshing the context which triggers the full Spring Framework initialization including registering shutdown hooks and calling the core refresh method.

After refresh completes, I'm firing the startup events and calling any extension points before finally signaling that the application is running.

Then I'm executing any ApplicationRunner and CommandLineRunner beans that were registered, firing the ready event, and returning the fully initialized context. Continuing through the rest of the startup sequence...

I'm checking if the context is running and notifying listeners that the application is ready, then handling any failures that occur during this final phase. The method returns the fully initialized context. I should note that Spring Boot 3.2+ introduced the bootstrap context creation as a separate step, whereas older versions handled it inline, and some methods like `configureIgnoreBeanInfo` have been removed in more recent versions.

content.text:
The source tree isn't present in this repo (only the `CLAUDE.md` is checked in). I'll give you the accurate call-chain from my knowledge of the Spring Boot 3.x source — every method, in order, from `run` to `context.refresh()`.

---

## Bootstrap call chain: `SpringApplication.run` → `context.refresh()`

### 1. Static entry point

```
SpringApplication.run(Class<?> primarySource, String... args)
  └─ new SpringApplication(primarySource)          // constructor
  └─ .run(args)                                    // instance run
```

**Constructor** (`SpringApplication(ResourceLoader, Class<?>...)`) does the setup work before `run` is called:
- `WebApplicationType.deduceFromClasspath()` — determines SERVLET / REACTIVE / NONE
- `getSpringFactoriesInstances(BootstrapRegistryInitializer.class)` — loads bootstrap initializers
- `getSpringFactoriesInstances(ApplicationContextInitializer.class)` — loads context initializers
- `getSpringFactoriesInstances(ApplicationListener.class)` — loads listeners
- `deduceMainApplicationClass()` — walks the stack to find the `main` method

---

### 2. Instance `run(String... args)` — the spine of bootstrap

```
run(String... args)
 ├─ Startup.create()
 ├─ createBootstrapContext()
 │    └─ iterates bootstrapRegistryInitializers → initializer.initialize(bootstrapContext)
 ├─ configureHeadlessProperty()
 ├─ getRunListeners(args)
 │    └─ getSpringFactoriesInstances(SpringApplicationRunListener.class, args)
 ├─ listeners.starting(bootstrapContext, mainApplicationClass)
 │    └─ EventPublishingRunListener.starting(...)
 │         └─ multicastInitialEvent(new ApplicationStartingEvent(...))
 │
 ├─ ── try ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 │
 ├─ new DefaultApplicationArguments(args)
 ├─ prepareEnvironment(listeners, bootstrapContext, applicationArguments)   ← see §3
 ├─ printBanner(environment)
 ├─ createApplicationContext()                                               ← see §4
 ├─ context.setApplicationStartup(applicationStartup)
 ├─ prepareContext(bootstrapContext, context, environment,
 │                 listeners, applicationArguments, printedBanner)           ← see §5
 ├─ refreshContext(context)                                                  ← see §6  ★
 ├─ afterRefresh(context, applicationArguments)          // empty by default
 ├─ startup.started(context, applicationArguments)
 ├─ listeners.started(context, startup.timeTakenToStarted())
 └─ callRunners(context, applicationArguments)
      ├─ context.getBeansOfType(ApplicationRunner.class)  → runner.run(args)
      └─ context.getBeansOfType(CommandLineRunner.class)  → runner.run(args)
```

---

### 3. `prepareEnvironment`

```
prepareEnvironment(listeners, bootstrapContext, applicationArguments)
 ├─ getOrCreateEnvironment()
 │    └─ applicationContextFactory.createEnvironment(webApplicationType)
 │         → ApplicationServletEnvironment  (servlet)
 │         → ApplicationReactiveWebEnvironment  (reactive)
 │         → ApplicationEnvironment  (none)
 ├─ configureEnvironment(environment, applicationArguments.getSourceArgs())
 │    ├─ configurePropertySources(environment, args)
 │    │    └─ adds SimpleCommandLinePropertySource if args present
 │    └─ configureProfiles(environment, args)
 ├─ ConfigurationPropertySources.attach(environment)   // relaxed-binding bridge
 ├─ listeners.environmentPrepared(bootstrapContext, environment)
 │    └─ EventPublishingRunListener → ApplicationEnvironmentPreparedEvent
 │         → ConfigDataEnvironmentPostProcessor.postProcessEnvironment(...)
 │              └─ new ConfigDataEnvironment(...).processAndApply()
 │                   // loads application.properties / application.yml,
 │                   // profile-specific files, config imports, etc.
 ├─ DefaultPropertiesPropertySource.moveToEnd(environment)
 └─ bindToSpringApplication(environment)
      └─ Binder.get(environment).bind("spring.main", Bindable.ofInstance(this))
```

---

### 4. `createApplicationContext`

```
createApplicationContext()
 └─ applicationContextFactory.create(webApplicationType)
      → AnnotationConfigServletWebServerApplicationContext   (servlet)
      → AnnotationConfigReactiveWebServerApplicationContext  (reactive)
      → AnnotationConfigApplicationContext                   (none)
```

Each of these constructors calls:
- `new AnnotatedBeanDefinitionReader(this)` — registers `ConfigurationClassPostProcessor`, `AutowiredAnnotationBeanPostProcessor`, etc. as infrastructure beans
- `new ClassPathBeanDefinitionScanner(this)`

---

### 5. `prepareContext`

```
prepareContext(bootstrapContext, context, environment, listeners, appArgs, banner)
 ├─ context.setEnvironment(environment)
 ├─ postProcessApplicationContext(context)
 │    ├─ context.setBeanNameGenerator(beanNameGenerator)   // if set
 │    ├─ context.setResourceLoader(resourceLoader)         // if set
 │    └─ context.getBeanFactory()
 │         .registerSingleton(SYSTEM_PROPERTY_BEAN_NAME, ...)
 ├─ addAotGeneratedInitializerIfNecessary(initializers)
 ├─ applyInitializers(context)
 │    └─ for each ApplicationContextInitializer: initializer.initialize(context)
 │         (e.g. DelegatingApplicationContextInitializer,
 │               SharedMetadataReaderFactoryContextInitializer,
 │               ContextIdApplicationContextInitializer, …)
 ├─ listeners.contextPrepared(context)
 │    └─ EventPublishingRunListener → ApplicationContextInitializedEvent
 ├─ bootstrapContext.close(context)
 │    └─ publishes BootstrapContextClosedEvent
 ├─ logStartupInfo / logStartupProfileInfo
 ├─ beanFactory.registerSingleton("springApplicationArguments", appArgs)
 ├─ beanFactory.registerSingleton("springBootBanner", banner)   // if present
 ├─ beanFactory.setAllowCircularReferences / setAllowBeanDefinitionOverriding
 ├─ load(context, sources)
 │    └─ new BeanDefinitionLoader(context, sources)
 │         └─ loader.load()
 │              └─ for each source: load(source)
 │                   → AnnotatedBeanDefinitionReader.register(source)
 │                      // registers the @SpringBootApplication class as a bean def
 └─ listeners.contextLoaded(context)
      └─ EventPublishingRunListener → ApplicationPreparedEvent
```

---

### 6. `refreshContext` → `context.refresh()` ★

```
refreshContext(context)
 └─ refresh(context)
      └─ context.refresh()           // AbstractApplicationContext.refresh()
```

`AbstractApplicationContext.refresh()` is pure Spring Framework — it's the 12-step sequence:
1. `prepareRefresh()` — sets start time, active flag, validates required properties
2. `obtainFreshBeanFactory()` — returns the `DefaultListableBeanFactory`
3. `prepareBeanFactory(beanFactory)` — registers `ApplicationContextAwareProcessor`, `Environment` beans, etc.
4. `postProcessBeanFactory(beanFactory)` — subclass hook (e.g. registers `ServletContextAwareProcessor` for web)
5. `invokeBeanFactoryPostProcessors(beanFactory)` — runs `ConfigurationClassPostProcessor` here, which parses `@Configuration`, `@ComponentScan`, `@Import`, `@Bean`; also runs `ConfigDataEnvironmentPostProcessor` etc.
6. `registerBeanPostProcessors(beanFactory)` — registers `AutowiredAnnotationBeanPostProcessor`, etc.
7. `initMessageSource()` — sets up i18n
8. `initApplicationEventMulticaster()`
9. `onRefresh()` — subclass hook; for web contexts, this calls `createWebServer()` which starts Tomcat/Jetty/Netty
10. `registerListeners()` — wires `ApplicationListener` beans to the multicaster
11. `finishBeanFactoryInitialization(beanFactory)` — instantiates all non-lazy singletons; this is where `@Autowired`, `@PostConstruct`, and `@Bean` methods run
12. `finishRefresh()` — clears caches, publishes `ContextRefreshedEvent`, starts `LifecycleProcessor` (which calls `SmartLifecycle.start()` on all lifecycle beans)

---

### Summary in one line per hop

| # | Caller | Callee |
|---|--------|--------|
| 1 | `SpringApplication.run(Class, String[])` | `new SpringApplication(...)` |
| 2 | `SpringApplication.run(Class, String[])` | `instance.run(String[])` |
| 3 | `run` | `createBootstrapContext()` |
| 4 | `run` | `configureHeadlessProperty()` |
| 5 | `run` | `getRunListeners(args)` |
| 6 | `run` | `listeners.starting(...)` |
| 7 | `run` | `prepareEnvironment(...)` |
| 8 | `prepareEnvironment` | `getOrCreateEnvironment()` |
| 9 | `prepareEnvironment` | `configureEnvironment(...)` |
| 10 | `configureEnvironment` | `configurePropertySources(...)` |
| 11 | `configureEnvironment` | `configureProfiles(...)` |
| 12 | `prepareEnvironment` | `listeners.environmentPrepared(...)` → loads config files |
| 13 | `prepareEnvironment` | `bindToSpringApplication(environment)` |
| 14 | `run` | `printBanner(environment)` |
| 15 | `run` | `createApplicationContext()` |
| 16 | `run` | `prepareContext(...)` |
| 17 | `prepareContext` | `postProcessApplicationContext(context)` |
| 18 | `prepareContext` | `applyInitializers(context)` |
| 19 | `prepareContext` | `listeners.contextPrepared(context)` |
| 20 | `prepareContext` | `bootstrapContext.close(context)` |
| 21 | `prepareContext` | `load(context, sources)` → registers primary source bean def |
| 22 | `prepareContext` | `listeners.contextLoaded(context)` |
| 23 | `run` | `refreshContext(context)` |
| 24 | `refreshContext` | `refresh(context)` |
| 25 | `refresh` | **`context.refresh()`** ← Spring Framework takes over here |

The `ConfigurationClassPostProcessor` (invoked at step 5 of `AbstractApplicationContext.refresh`) is what expands your `@SpringBootApplication` class into the full bean graph — scanning components, processing `@Import`, and triggering auto-configuration via `@EnableAutoConfiguration`.

result:
The source tree isn't present in this repo (only the `CLAUDE.md` is checked in). I'll give you the accurate call-chain from my knowledge of the Spring Boot 3.x source — every method, in order, from `run` to `context.refresh()`.

---

## Bootstrap call chain: `SpringApplication.run` → `context.refresh()`

### 1. Static entry point

```
SpringApplication.run(Class<?> primarySource, String... args)
  └─ new SpringApplication(primarySource)          // constructor
  └─ .run(args)                                    // instance run
```

**Constructor** (`SpringApplication(ResourceLoader, Class<?>...)`) does the setup work before `run` is called:
- `WebApplicationType.deduceFromClasspath()` — determines SERVLET / REACTIVE / NONE
- `getSpringFactoriesInstances(BootstrapRegistryInitializer.class)` — loads bootstrap initializers
- `getSpringFactoriesInstances(ApplicationContextInitializer.class)` — loads context initializers
- `getSpringFactoriesInstances(ApplicationListener.class)` — loads listeners
- `deduceMainApplicationClass()` — walks the stack to find the `main` method

---

### 2. Instance `run(String... args)` — the spine of bootstrap

```
run(String... args)
 ├─ Startup.create()
 ├─ createBootstrapContext()
 │    └─ iterates bootstrapRegistryInitializers → initializer.initialize(bootstrapContext)
 ├─ configureHeadlessProperty()
 ├─ getRunListeners(args)
 │    └─ getSpringFactoriesInstances(SpringApplicationRunListener.class, args)
 ├─ listeners.starting(bootstrapContext, mainApplicationClass)
 │    └─ EventPublishingRunListener.starting(...)
 │         └─ multicastInitialEvent(new ApplicationStartingEvent(...))
 │
 ├─ ── try ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 │
 ├─ new DefaultApplicationArguments(args)
 ├─ prepareEnvironment(listeners, bootstrapContext, applicationArguments)   ← see §3
 ├─ printBanner(environment)
 ├─ createApplicationContext()                                               ← see §4
 ├─ context.setApplicationStartup(applicationStartup)
 ├─ prepareContext(bootstrapContext, context, environment,
 │                 listeners, applicationArguments, printedBanner)           ← see §5
 ├─ refreshContext(context)                                                  ← see §6  ★
 ├─ afterRefresh(context, applicationArguments)          // empty by default
 ├─ startup.started(context, applicationArguments)
 ├─ listeners.started(context, startup.timeTakenToStarted())
 └─ callRunners(context, applicationArguments)
      ├─ context.getBeansOfType(ApplicationRunner.class)  → runner.run(args)
      └─ context.getBeansOfType(CommandLineRunner.class)  → runner.run(args)
```

---

### 3. `prepareEnvironment`

```
prepareEnvironment(listeners, bootstrapContext, applicationArguments)
 ├─ getOrCreateEnvironment()
 │    └─ applicationContextFactory.createEnvironment(webApplicationType)
 │         → ApplicationServletEnvironment  (servlet)
 │         → ApplicationReactiveWebEnvironment  (reactive)
 │         → ApplicationEnvironment  (none)
 ├─ configureEnvironment(environment, applicationArguments.getSourceArgs())
 │    ├─ configurePropertySources(environment, args)
 │    │    └─ adds SimpleCommandLinePropertySource if args present
 │    └─ configureProfiles(environment, args)
 ├─ ConfigurationPropertySources.attach(environment)   // relaxed-binding bridge
 ├─ listeners.environmentPrepared(bootstrapContext, environment)
 │    └─ EventPublishingRunListener → ApplicationEnvironmentPreparedEvent
 │         → ConfigDataEnvironmentPostProcessor.postProcessEnvironment(...)
 │              └─ new ConfigDataEnvironment(...).processAndApply()
 │                   // loads application.properties / application.yml,
 │                   // profile-specific files, config imports, etc.
 ├─ DefaultPropertiesPropertySource.moveToEnd(environment)
 └─ bindToSpringApplication(environment)
      └─ Binder.get(environment).bind("spring.main", Bindable.ofInstance(this))
```

---

### 4. `createApplicationContext`

```
createApplicationContext()
 └─ applicationContextFactory.create(webApplicationType)
      → AnnotationConfigServletWebServerApplicationContext   (servlet)
      → AnnotationConfigReactiveWebServerApplicationContext  (reactive)
      → AnnotationConfigApplicationContext                   (none)
```

Each of these constructors calls:
- `new AnnotatedBeanDefinitionReader(this)` — registers `ConfigurationClassPostProcessor`, `AutowiredAnnotationBeanPostProcessor`, etc. as infrastructure beans
- `new ClassPathBeanDefinitionScanner(this)`

---

### 5. `prepareContext`

```
prepareContext(bootstrapContext, context, environment, listeners, appArgs, banner)
 ├─ context.setEnvironment(environment)
 ├─ postProcessApplicationContext(context)
 │    ├─ context.setBeanNameGenerator(beanNameGenerator)   // if set
 │    ├─ context.setResourceLoader(resourceLoader)         // if set
 │    └─ context.getBeanFactory()
 │         .registerSingleton(SYSTEM_PROPERTY_BEAN_NAME, ...)
 ├─ addAotGeneratedInitializerIfNecessary(initializers)
 ├─ applyInitializers(context)
 │    └─ for each ApplicationContextInitializer: initializer.initialize(context)
 │         (e.g. DelegatingApplicationContextInitializer,
 │               SharedMetadataReaderFactoryContextInitializer,
 │               ContextIdApplicationContextInitializer, …)
 ├─ listeners.contextPrepared(context)
 │    └─ EventPublishingRunListener → ApplicationContextInitializedEvent
 ├─ bootstrapContext.close(context)
 │    └─ publishes BootstrapContextClosedEvent
 ├─ logStartupInfo / logStartupProfileInfo
 ├─ beanFactory.registerSingleton("springApplicationArguments", appArgs)
 ├─ beanFactory.registerSingleton("springBootBanner", banner)   // if present
 ├─ beanFactory.setAllowCircularReferences / setAllowBeanDefinitionOverriding
 ├─ load(context, sources)
 │    └─ new BeanDefinitionLoader(context, sources)
 │         └─ loader.load()
 │              └─ for each source: load(source)
 │                   → AnnotatedBeanDefinitionReader.register(source)
 │                      // registers the @SpringBootApplication class as a bean def
 └─ listeners.contextLoaded(context)
      └─ EventPublishingRunListener → ApplicationPreparedEvent
```

---

### 6. `refreshContext` → `context.refresh()` ★

```
refreshContext(context)
 └─ refresh(context)
      └─ context.refresh()           // AbstractApplicationContext.refresh()
```

`AbstractApplicationContext.refresh()` is pure Spring Framework — it's the 12-step sequence:
1. `prepareRefresh()` — sets start time, active flag, validates required properties
2. `obtainFreshBeanFactory()` — returns the `DefaultListableBeanFactory`
3. `prepareBeanFactory(beanFactory)` — registers `ApplicationContextAwareProcessor`, `Environment` beans, etc.
4. `postProcessBeanFactory(beanFactory)` — subclass hook (e.g. registers `ServletContextAwareProcessor` for web)
5. `invokeBeanFactoryPostProcessors(beanFactory)` — runs `ConfigurationClassPostProcessor` here, which parses `@Configuration`, `@ComponentScan`, `@Import`, `@Bean`; also runs `ConfigDataEnvironmentPostProcessor` etc.
6. `registerBeanPostProcessors(beanFactory)` — registers `AutowiredAnnotationBeanPostProcessor`, etc.
7. `initMessageSource()` — sets up i18n
8. `initApplicationEventMulticaster()`
9. `onRefresh()` — subclass hook; for web contexts, this calls `createWebServer()` which starts Tomcat/Jetty/Netty
10. `registerListeners()` — wires `ApplicationListener` beans to the multicaster
11. `finishBeanFactoryInitialization(beanFactory)` — instantiates all non-lazy singletons; this is where `@Autowired`, `@PostConstruct`, and `@Bean` methods run
12. `finishRefresh()` — clears caches, publishes `ContextRefreshedEvent`, starts `LifecycleProcessor` (which calls `SmartLifecycle.start()` on all lifecycle beans)

---

### Summary in one line per hop

| # | Caller | Callee |
|---|--------|--------|
| 1 | `SpringApplication.run(Class, String[])` | `new SpringApplication(...)` |
| 2 | `SpringApplication.run(Class, String[])` | `instance.run(String[])` |
| 3 | `run` | `createBootstrapContext()` |
| 4 | `run` | `configureHeadlessProperty()` |
| 5 | `run` | `getRunListeners(args)` |
| 6 | `run` | `listeners.starting(...)` |
| 7 | `run` | `prepareEnvironment(...)` |
| 8 | `prepareEnvironment` | `getOrCreateEnvironment()` |
| 9 | `prepareEnvironment` | `configureEnvironment(...)` |
| 10 | `configureEnvironment` | `configurePropertySources(...)` |
| 11 | `configureEnvironment` | `configureProfiles(...)` |
| 12 | `prepareEnvironment` | `listeners.environmentPrepared(...)` → loads config files |
| 13 | `prepareEnvironment` | `bindToSpringApplication(environment)` |
| 14 | `run` | `printBanner(environment)` |
| 15 | `run` | `createApplicationContext()` |
| 16 | `run` | `prepareContext(...)` |
| 17 | `prepareContext` | `postProcessApplicationContext(context)` |
| 18 | `prepareContext` | `applyInitializers(context)` |
| 19 | `prepareContext` | `listeners.contextPrepared(context)` |
| 20 | `prepareContext` | `bootstrapContext.close(context)` |
| 21 | `prepareContext` | `load(context, sources)` → registers primary source bean def |
| 22 | `prepareContext` | `listeners.contextLoaded(context)` |
| 23 | `run` | `refreshContext(context)` |
| 24 | `refreshContext` | `refresh(context)` |
| 25 | `refresh` | **`context.refresh()`** ← Spring Framework takes over here |

The `ConfigurationClassPostProcessor` (invoked at step 5 of `AbstractApplicationContext.refresh`) is what expands your `@SpringBootApplication` class into the full bean graph — scanning components, processing `@Import`, and triggering auto-configuration via `@EnableAutoConfiguration`.
